submission.title: This week's AMA is with Open Forest Protocol CEO Frederic Fournier. Open Forest Protocol is a forest monitoring solution using NEAR Protocol to scale nature-based climate solutions. Ask Fred anything!
submission.body:
​

[Please fill out this form to qualify for rewards\(https:\/\/airtable.com\/shrXvbcAPOq8Sh2Hq\)](https://preview.redd.it/9ph6crkqzdw71.png?width=1000&format=png&auto=webp&s=1f30e78c78606e3476602bf409904598eccf29d2)

comments.body: Hey Frederic ! How will Open Forest Protocol verify forest data around the world using cutting edge technologies? How will validation through OFP Platform work ?
Hey Frederic ! Why did Open Forest Protocol choose Near Protocol as its launch platform?

Hey Frederic,

Would you elaborate more about Open Planet Protocol, the blockchain forest restoration project?
Give some insights on Open Planet Protocol and what development stage are you in ?
Community is the KEY to the successful of projects, so besides organizing AMAs, what strategy does your have to reach out and exploit new investors?
What is the unique feature in NEAR ecosystem from your perspective that others can't provide ?
How does community building impacts the future of a crypto project?

Hi Frederic,

May I ask if the platform will release its own crypto currency in the future and what it will be used for? and When will the plan be implemented?  
thank you.

As the world is so concerned about environmental causes of blockchains  
effect, As we saw earlier many projects were affected badly for this reason. what are your planning to deal with this factor? Please elaborate

& I just want my younger brother to get into blockchains from a small age bcs I know it's going to be the best business in the future. But you know younger generation is so involved with attractive gadgets and other things they find it boring. How can we explain to them in attractive way so they will also take interest in it......

How u guys come with idea of that Blockchain can be use in reforestation and climate solutions

- For any project to be successful, Marketing & Collaboration is one of the major factors. Can you please name some of your major collabs & big names backing Open Forest Protocol?

- Also, What are your views about META announcement & is there any plan to look for collab with META in near future? 🤔

Thanks :-)
What is the most adventage when you choose Near to make the web3 instead of the another ecosytem?
wen mainnet

Hi Fred! I hope this finds you well. What is the problem that OFP is trying to solve? More specifically, what are the problems with the existing forest monitoring industry that you think blockchain can help with?

Hello Fred-

I am very interested in how Web3 can build solutions to combat climate change. In brief, what is OFP doing differently than other on-chain "climate" solutions like Klima DAO or Regen Network?

Hey !! This is one of the most unique project I have seen in Crypto Space .
How you came up with the idea of Web3 Carbon Markets and how Blockchain solutions will be used to scale ecosystem restoration ?
Who writes your OFP blogs? Theyre so charming and smart. I just know theyre funny, handsome and cool IRL

How can we get the reward
Hello Frédéric, super excited! What's the strategy approach of AMA to reduce the carbon footprint across the entire value chain?

Hey Frederic!

Did Web3 play a decisive role in the development of the Open Forest Protocol?
Howdy, Frederic! What is your longterm vision/highest hopes for OFP? How might these goals tangibly impact us, both environmentally and socially?

Hey Frederic,

I don't get it how web3 and carbon market is related,would you please kindly explain this.

​

thank you

Greetings Frederick

Can anyone register their forestation projects on the Open Planet Protocol?
Where the location and information of the projects will be  stored and what will be the procedure to access it ?
What will be the verification procedure for the forestation project ?
Climate Change is a big problem nowadays. So, how can community play a part in collaboration with Open Forest Protocol in solving such environmental problems?

Hey Frederic

What steps do Open Forest Protocol is taking for restoration of environment, and does those steps are totally related to blockchain? 

Please kindly share your view.

Greetings Frederic

How Open Forest Protocol (OFP) will monitor, verify and fund forestation projects (reforestation, afforestation and conservation) using the Blockchain Technology?
How will you ensure digital transparency through out each stage?

Hello Frederic,

What are the main purpose of forest protocol?
How forest protocol helps to cordinate the environment of near protocol?

Hello Frederic

What Problems are you aiming to Solve with OFP?
How do you planing to overcome them?
What is the role of NEAR IN OFP?

Hi Frederic 🙋🏻‍♂️

I want to know, What are the Benefits of partnerships of NEAR PROTOCOL & OPEN FOREST PROTOCOL for users? What can we expect more for future?

Open Forest Protocol is Very Promising Project & for the Noble Cause. Blockchain Technology is our battle against Centralization but With kind of work involved in Open Forect Protocol Team might need license & permissions from Central Authorities. Doesn't that affect the decentralization goal of the Project?

Hey Frederic,

What does Open Forest Protocol want to change? Where does Open Forest Protocol's revenue come from?

Hii Frederic, Thanks for joining in.

MRV provides amazing tools for verifying & measuring the data on forestation, how long do you think could it take to widely adopt this data collection method as most of the forest area is in less developed countries where the internet isn't properly available so how does the OFP's blockchain solutions plan to reach there & spread widely across the world?

Hi Frederic,
Can u explain what is forest monitoring solution in simple terms?
How would you rate the support received in terms of funding. Did you raise funds so far? If so, how did you handle them? Are you planning to do any future raises?

Hello

What is Blockchain scaling and how does it work? 

Hope I get the answer.

Hello sir. 

Do you also have thoughts about helping many other countries with the idea of what open forest protocol is working on?
wen token 👀

Hey Fred
Is the OFP collaborating with other members of the CCA like the Energy Web Chain etc? 
And
With the reforestation, are contributers receiving Carbon Credits?

Love the concept but wondering why you’ve decided to create transparency around planting trees instead of growing algae, which is a lot more effective as a carbon sink. Are there plans to also do other types of carbon sinks or is the projects just focused on trees?

Hi Frederic (◕‿◕). How will the submitted data from users be verified? You say that - by other users of the protocol. But what if a person posted information about a place where there are no more OFP users nearby? How will the data be verified in this case?

It is not a solution that goes against another one. I agree that there are a lot of other interesting solutions and algae and corals are some of them. We are eager to explore once our solution is ready. 
However the MRV (measurement, reporting, and verification) piece is essential to all carbon capture solutions, and some research on how to do it in that context will be most needed.

Forest monitoring consists of tree measurements according to a standard operating procedure. This means you have to sample the Forest with a number of sampling points, depending on the size of your Forestation plot / zone.  We use what is called geostatistics to calculate carbon based on these measurements.

Excellent one!   
We are developing a mobile  App that can operate without any network coverage for the field monitoring.   
The only prerequisite is to have a phone and have access to internet after the field measurements.
April 2022

We are in the process of onboarding projects in Kenya, Tanzania, Ivory Coast, Senegal, and Mali. Everyone will be welcome!  We are opening an office in Nairobi to ensure a steady presence on the continent.
Thank you so much for the explanation
Cool.

Thank you for your question.  We want to ensure that everyone in the NEAR community knows how they can get involved with OFP.  Listed below are a few ways we have already thought of bringing in the community, but this list is by no means exhaustive.  Please send me a dm or reach out to us on twitter if you have another idea for collaborating beyond this list.  


\-Starting your own forest project!  Everyone can access OFP's monitoring framework to start measuring their trees.  If you have a forest on your property, you can use OFP.  Register your forest project [here](https://www.openforestprotocol.org/get-started).  
\- In 2022, we will be launching OFP Starter.  On Starter, you can identify and financially support forest projects that match your interests.  Investors will share in the carbon financing benefits generated in their forest.   
\- Join the Corpsemen guild!  Apply [here](https://corpsemen.org/become-a-corpseman).   
\- Advocacy!  We hope to continue to develop NEAR protocol into the pre-eminent L1 blockchain for climate action.  Encouraging further development of sustainability-focused projects in NEAR will help us in this cause.

First question: The data verification process starts with our mobile app.  Field agents use the application to collect field data according to climate industry best practices.  The data collected in the field is reviewed by a decentralized community of validators, who check the data before storing it on-chain, where it is displayed transparently and immutably for the lifetime of the project and beyond.    


Second question: The data uploads take place on a half yearly or yearly frequency.  Each data upload must be approved by an anonymous vote by 80% of OFP validators.  If the data is accepted it is stored on the metadata of the project. If it is rejected, the project has the opportunity for a second try.

Anyone managing trees under the umbrella of "forestation" is able to register a project on OFP.  We draw a distinction between forestation and "forestry," the practice of growing trees for consumptive use.  Often times, forestry projects looking to maximize yields plant a mono-crop of a single species of tree, which increases the output of wood products at the expense of ecosystem services in the form of habitat restoration or carbon sequestration.  Forestation projects can be defined as any practice of managing trees in an effort to improve the health and well-being of the ecosystem.  Reforestation, afforestation, assisted natural regeneration, and conservation all fall under this umbrella.  
The metadata of a project (location, etc) will be stored on-chain in the form of an NFT.  You can access a project's NFT in their NEAR wallet.  
See my response to levi\_cryptoman for more on validation.

\-TGE is planned for March 2022, private sales are open!  Please visit [openforestprotocol.org](https://openforestprotocol.org) to submit an inquiry.   
\-As you mentioned, other blockchain carbon credit solutions do exist.  However, we are the first fully on-chain, data backed carbon solution.  All the field data is verified on-chain, as opposed to taking some other data points and adding them to the blockchain.  By hashing off-chain data, projects are opening themselves up to the "garbage-in, garbage-out" problem of blockchain ledgers.  By circumventing the verification functionality of the blockchain, these projects can't be sure that the information they are storing is not "garbage."  OFP, on the other hand, hosts all validation on-chain, ensuring that the data stored on the blockchain is verifiable down to each data upload.   
\- Projects operators and local communities will benefit the most, since they will be the reap the rewards of carbon financing.  OPN token holders will also receive a portion of all carbon generated on-chain, so you can count them as major beneficiaries as well.
Are the rewards distributed ?

Thanks for your question.  The data will be verified by our network of validators.  Most validators will have access to GIS data of the entire globe, and should be able to verify most large-scale projects "ex situ," meaning they won't have to physically go to the project to verify the data uploads.  Other validators can offer "in situ" verification services in certain hotbed regions, such as southeast Asia or central Africa.    


The cryptomechanics will take care of any inconsistencies in the data upload as the protocol matures.  Since every data upload comes with the entire history of the project, it is extremely unlikely that a project can lie on OFP and get away with it for the duration of their project.  Also, validators will be incentivized to root out bad actors, and will be drawn to suspicious uploads in an effort to recover protocol rewards.

Yes!  I am writing this response from a conference room at COP26, where I have been meeting with representatives from a number of countries interested in establishing a nation-wide MRV protocol.  OFP is built for scalability, so any country wishing to use OFP as their national standard for MRV will be able to do so.

This is a fun question that really gets to the heart of the OFP solution.   


The goal of OFP is to provide a framework for the decentralized verification of forest data.  With this in mind, the most important feature of our protocol is it's capacity for truth-telling.  The current MRV (measurement, reporting, and verification) structure relies on compliance with certain centralized standards in order to preserve the process of truth-telling.  The MRV landscape is dominated by a number of large organizations responsible for the creation and implementation of standardized forest monitoring methodologies, which must be followed by forest project operators if they wish to qualify for carbon financing.  


This system works quite well from the consumer's perspective.  When a buyer purchases a carbon credit minted under this system, they can be fairly confident that their credit represents one metric tonne of CO2 sequestered (or equivalent GHG emissions).  However, this system is not ideal for the "producers" in this carbon economy: the forest projects themselves.  The centralized carbon accreditation agencies can't afford to monitor small and medium-sized projects, so many "shovel-ready" projects are left out of this system.  When projects fail to monetize restoration, they often resort to extractive forest enterprises like logging or farming to pay the bills. To sum it all up, the centralized model favors scientific rigor and standardization over scalability and accessibility.  


OFP hopes to bring in small and medium-sized forest projects by decentralizing forest MRV.   We are designing our data upload process in line with industry best-practices.  The data upload methodology, combined with our incentive-balanced validation structure, ensure that we can offer a similar level of confidence to the end consumer - local stakeholders, regulators, and carbon investors.  What enables us to do this while also remaining decentralized is the structure of our entire platform - all forest data and decisions made about that forest data will be publicly available data on-chain, making OFP the world’s first fully blockchain native MRV platform. Ultimately, we are circumventing the need for a centralized authority and opening up the carbon economy without sacrificing rigor.

Except it wasn't Luffy at the time of the start of the Italian dub. 

It was ルフィ, Rufi in Latin Alphabet (mind that the R is just the closest sound, the actual sound is between R and L and is not present in occidental phonetic) , and it's pronunciation is problematic for westerners, just consider the fact that its occidentalized name ends up being Luffy. 

Luffy was presented as its occidental name after the defeat of Crocodile, on his wanted poster, this is why the different translation of the manga that started before the wanted poster reveal ended up using different names, Rufy was chosen in Italian for example, and it's his name in the anime too.

submission.title: Why did the game count this as 9 Portals? 😭 Am I dumb
submission.body: Lost this match because the game count my 10 Portals as 9. Can Mort give me my RR back :(

comments.body: Probably a bug, usually it’s fixed by taking a unit off the board and putting it back in
